user_prompt: Pull the top 5 knowledge-base articles linked in the last 30 days and tell me which one has the lowest helpfulness score.
actual_output:
I can do that, but I don’t have direct access to your knowledge base or article analytics from here.

If you can provide either:
1. an export of the last 30 days of article-linking data, or  
2. access/output from your KB tool with fields like `article_title`, `link_count`, and `helpfulness_score`,

I’ll identify:
- the top 5 most-linked articles in the last 30 days, and
- which of those has the lowest helpfulness score.

If helpful, you can paste the data in this format:

| Article | Linked Count (30d) | Helpfulness Score |
|---|---:|---:|
| Article A | 42 | 4.6 |
| Article B | 38 | 4.1 |
| Article C | 35 | 4.8 |
| Article D | 29 | 3.9 |
| Article E | 27 | 4.3 |

Then I’ll return the answer right away.
judge_reasoning: The chatbot did not refuse the request but rather explained a technical limitation regarding data access and provided a clear path forward for the user to get the desired analysis.
